nodes.name: Sheet-Pan Pork Dinner — Maple-Kissed Skillet
nodes.description: name: Sheet-Pan Pork Dinner — Maple-Kissed Skillet | short_description: One-pan sheet-pan dinner: pork tenderloin roasted with potatoes and green beans in olive oil for a simple, balanced meal. | meal
nodes.premise: Meal: Sheet-Pan Pork Dinner — Maple-Kissed Skillet - name: Sheet-Pan Pork Dinner — Maple-Kissed Skillet | short_description: One-pan sheet-pan dinner: pork tenderloin roasted with potatoes and green beans in olive oil for a simple, balanced meal. | meal_suggestion: Preheat oven to 425°F (220°C). Toss potatoes and green beans with olive oil, salt, and pepper, and spread on a sheet pan. Rub pork tenderloin with herbs and a light coat of oil, place on the pan. Roast 25–30 minutes until the pork reaches 145°F (63°C) and the potatoes are tender. Rest pork 3–5 minutes, then slice and serve with the vegetables. | nutrition_note: Lean pork tenderloin provides high-quality protein; potatoes offer complex carbohydrates; green beans add fiber and micronutrients; olive oil contributes heart-healthy fats. For a lighter version, use minimal added salt and portion appropriately. Let the pork rest after cooking to retain juiciness.

nodes.name: Roast Chicken Dinner — Garden Veg Combo
nodes.description: name: Roast Chicken Dinner — Garden Veg Combo | short_description: A balanced roast dinner with protein-rich chicken, wholesome quinoa, and colorful garden vegetables. | meal_suggestion: Roast chicken
nodes.premise: Meal: Roast Chicken Dinner — Garden Veg Combo - name: Roast Chicken Dinner — Garden Veg Combo | short_description: A balanced roast dinner with protein-rich chicken, wholesome quinoa, and colorful garden vegetables. | meal_suggestion: Roast chicken thighs (skinless if preferred), cook quinoa, and steam or roast broccoli and carrots. Plate together and finish with a squeeze of lemon and a light drizzle of olive oil for flavor. | nutrition_note: Protein from chicken thighs; quinoa adds complete protein and fiber; broccoli and carrots provide vitamins, minerals, and additional fiber. For a leaner option, use skinless thighs.

nodes.name: Teriyaki Chicken Dinner — Maple-Kissed Bake
nodes.description: name: Teriyaki Chicken Dinner — Maple-Kissed Bake | short_description: Baked chicken with mixed vegetables and rice glazed in teriyaki sauce for a balanced, savory dinner. | meal_suggestion: Meal prep
nodes.premise: Meal: Teriyaki Chicken Dinner — Maple-Kissed Bake - name: Teriyaki Chicken Dinner — Maple-Kissed Bake | short_description: Baked chicken with mixed vegetables and rice glazed in teriyaki sauce for a balanced, savory dinner. | meal_suggestion: Meal prep idea: bake a larger batch and portion into 4 servings. For more fiber, swap white rice for brown rice or quinoa. Opt for low-sodium teriyaki sauce or use less sauce. Add sesame seeds and green onions for flavor, and pair with a small side of fruit or leafy greens to enhance balance. | nutrition_note: Protein from chicken supports satiety and muscle maintenance; mixed vegetables add fiber, vitamins, and minerals; rice provides energy from carbohydrates. Teriyaki sauce adds flavor but can raise sodium and added sugar; consider using low-sodium teriyaki or moderating sauce amount. For more fiber, substitute brown rice and/or increase vegetable portions.
